title: Type specifiers should be listed in a standard order
description: Type specifiers should be listed in a standard orderAnalyze your codeconsistency-conventionalmaintainabilityCode SmellcppcoreguidelinesWhy is this an issue?More InfoShared coding conventions allow teams to collaborate efficiently. This rule checks that type specifiers always appear in the following order:typedeftype name, spelling of built-in types with more than one type-specifier:signedness -signedorunsignedlast single type-specifier orshort intlong intlong long intlong doubleSince the positioning of theconstkeyword is controversial, this rule does not check it.Noncompliant code exampleint typedef T; double long d; char unsigned ch; long signed int i;Compliant solutiontypedef int T; long double d; unsigned char ch; signed long int i;Available In:Catch issues on the fly,in your IDEDetect
recommendation: Review and fix according to the rule guidelines.

checks.message: Type specifiers should be listed in a standard order. Review and fix according to the rule guidelines.
